description_long: This is a large, modern military or utility helicopter with a streamlined fuselage and a prominent main rotor system featuring five long, dark blades. The body is painted in a muted gray-blue color, with angular cockpit windows at the front and a slightly raised tail boom ending in a tail rotor. The helicopter has retractable landing gear rather than traditional skids, and the side doors and hatches are visible along the fuselage. The cockpit windows are multi-paneled and slightly tinted, providing wide visibility for the pilots. The overall design suggests a robust, functional aircraft suitable for transport or tactical operations in urban environments.
description: A large, gray-blue helicopter with five main rotor blades, angular cockpit windows, and retractable landing gear, designed for utility or military use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, multi-paneled cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the five main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, main rotor, tail boom, and the landing gear beneath the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the main rotor blades from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit and side hatches.
attribute_rotor_blades: Five long, straight main rotor blades made of composite material, colored black with light tips, each blade approximately 8-9 meters in length, no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present, with four short blades arranged in a cross configuration, mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored black with light tips.
attribute_landing_skids: Not present; the helicopter uses retractable landing gear with visible wheels beneath the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, angular cockpit windows at the front, slightly tinted, framed in black, providing wide forward and downward visibility.
